text.title: Tailwind CSS: Mandatory Rules
text.summary: Non-negotiable rules for content glob configuration, mobile-first breakpoints, no hardcoded hex, @apply restrictions, and v3 vs v4 config format.

rule.statement: Configure the `content` array explicitly in tailwind.config.ts (v3) or @source directives (v4). Include all template sources: src/, components/, MDX, and server-side templates. Never rely on autodetection.
rule.rationale: JIT only scans paths inside content globs. Classes outside the glob are purged silently — the component renders without styles and there is no build error.

rule.statement: Write base styles for mobile with no prefix, then use `sm:`, `md:`, `lg:`, `xl:` to scale up. Never write desktop-only styles and use max-* breakpoints to override down.
rule.rationale: Mobile-first produces additive, conflict-free breakpoint chains. Overriding down with max-* creates specificity tangles and requires more CSS classes to maintain.

rule.statement: All color values must come from design tokens defined in tailwind.config.ts `theme.extend.colors` (v3) or `@theme` block (v4). Never write arbitrary hex values like `bg-[#2563eb]` in component files.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded hex in components defeats centralized token management. When brand colors change, every component file needs a grep-and-replace instead of a single config edit.

rule.statement: Limit `@apply` to thin reset helpers in globals.css with at most 3 utilities per rule. Abstractions that need more than 3 utilities belong in React/Vue components, not in CSS.
rule.rationale: Long @apply chains recreate the maintenance problem that component extraction solves. Agents fed "@apply is fine" produce sprawling stylesheet abstractions instead of composable components.

rule.statement: For v3: use tailwind.config.ts with `theme.extend` and `plugins: [require(...)]`. For v4: use a CSS-first `@theme` block in globals.css; do not write a tailwind.config.ts. Never mix APIs.
rule.rationale: Training data mixes v3 and v4 examples. A v3-style config.ts is silently ignored by v4; a @theme block in CSS is not processed by v3. Specifying the version prevents silent misconfiguration.

rule.statement: Install `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` and configure it in `.prettierrc`. Enforce class order in CI with `prettier --check`. Never commit hand-sorted class strings.
rule.rationale: Different agents produce different class orders. Canonical order makes diffs readable and prevents spurious merge conflicts in class attribute lines.

rule.statement: Add every Tailwind plugin to the `plugins` array in tailwind.config.ts (v3) or import it with `@plugin` (v4) before using its utility classes. Never assume plugins are auto-registered.
rule.rationale: Plugins like @tailwindcss/forms are not bundled — they must be explicitly registered. Missing registration produces no build error; the classes are silently absent at runtime.
